parsed_review.summary: CP00-913 RuleEngine HRX slice tail (RP30.P02.M06.S02-S11, risk A, 10 implementation units) is a sound descriptor-only pack. The CP913 binding, requirements, descriptor/case-set generator (packages/hrx/src/registry.js), validators (packages/hrx/src/validators.js), tests (packages/hrx/test/model.test.js), the serialized contract projection (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json), and the validate script (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs) are mutually consistent. They enforce that rule definitions, rule execution runtime, rule decision writes, HR AI final judgment, payroll runtime, permission/audit/product-state writes, runtime receipts, separate-product creation, credentials/secrets, and real employee/candidate/payroll/document data all stay closed at the row, baseline, domain-entity, and attestation levels. Plan<->binding<->manifest<->contract agree (range, 10 units, risk A, next pack CP00-914 / RP30.P02.M06.S12), so deviation_from_plan:false is accurate. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read_only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), human approval is required before runtime opening (deferred to CP00-914), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and the Hermes H30 packet emits_runtime_receipt:false (used only for deterministic control-plane review/evidence handling, not product runtime). Review-hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded with valid JSON (stdout 1455 bytes, exit 0) and no invalid/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped review evidence is being counted as valid (pack_level_claude_review status pending, my review is the one being produced). The pack-level production_ready claim is the descriptor-only flag hrx_rule_engine_tail_descriptor_verified; runtime_ready remains false and is supported by the evidence. Provenance note: the validation command exit codes (rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate, node --check/--test) are reported from command-evidence.json; re-execution is outside read-only scope, but static code/contract consistency I verified directly corroborates them. One non-blocking P3 on closeout commit hygiene.

findings.title: Unrelated tracked dirty files present in worktree at closeout
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json lists two tracked, modified files unrelated to CP00-913 in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved: scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html (both status ' M'). These belong to the separate non-CP UI workstream, not the HRX rule-engine pack. A broad `git add -A` / `git commit -a` for the closeout commit (commit_policy.message 'Close CP00-913 HRX rule engine tail pack') would bundle these unrelated changes into the pack commit. This does not affect the descriptor-only boundaries or the production_ready claim of CP00-913.
findings.recommendation: Stage only pack-scoped paths for the closeout commit: packages/hrx/**, contracts/hrx-people-contract.json, scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-913/**, and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-913/**. Leave the progress-control-room UI files to the separate non-CP workstream.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory only; does not block pack or goal closeout. Resolve by scoping commit staging to pack files.
